name: Filigree
description: A physarum (slime-mold) agent network whose cell structure tracks the music's energy: loud passages divide the gold web into a fine, busy lattice of many small cells; quiet passages merge it into a few large, calm cells. ~262K agents deposit onto a diffusing trail map (Jones / Bleuje / Sage-Jenson model); continuous energy drives the merge/divide, with a per-beat pulse on the kick. Kintsugi palette: gold veins on pure black. A loose energy-accompaniment (continuous-energy coupling, not beat-locked — the trail substrate can't carry tight event-sync; tightly-synced cell division is the separate reaction-diffusion preset). Graduated from the throwaway physarum sketch; certified PHYS.5.
author: Matt
